channel.title: My self is steam: Giuseppe Cocomazzi's blog
channel.description: Insights into computer security, programming and math

item.title: Keep It Like a Secret: Finality and trust in commit-reveal protocols
item.description: "Trustless" bridges claim to allow secure asset exchange across chains even in the presence of malicious actors. However, we argue that truly trustless protocols based on commit-reveal schemes are actually impossible when at least one of the participants does not wait for block finality.

item.title: Subtleties of BLS Signatures
item.description: This article explores an observation in the use of BLS signature aggregation within a production blockchain.

item.title: Hash What You Mean
item.description: Vulnerabilities in Merkle tree implementation used for hashing collections of objects, with an application to Omni Network.

item.title: Proof of Nothing
item.description: Inductive Reasoning Considered Harmful for blockchain light clients.

item.title: Security Advisories for CometBFT
item.description: Missing BLS12-381 Public Key Validation, and BLS12-381 Wrong Signature Generation in CometBFT

item.title: Ghost in the Block: Ethereum Consensus Vulnerability
Timing Side Channel in Hashicorp Vault Shamir's Secret Sharing
The CoreDNS Cache Poisoning Conjecture
item.description: CoreDNS might be vulnerable to cache poisoning attacks.

item.title: Timing Attack Side Channel in IAIK JCE DSA Implementation
item.description: How to find side channel leaks in Java Cryptography Extension providers.

item.title: Mindreading your Ps and Qs
item.description: Statistical inference of Chebyshev's bias in RSA numbers.

item.title: Finding Duplicate RSA Moduli in the Wild
item.description: Duplicate RSA moduli census and bash interpreter abuse in the way of Diogenes.

item.title: SQL Injection Suminagashi
item.description: Exploiting SQL injections when error messages are the only output.

item.title: Hacking Safenet MobilePass OTP Token
item.description: A Secret You Own is a Secret You Pwn.

item.title: Programming had Troy won the war
GNU APL on OpenBSD
item.description: Porting of GNU APL to OpenBSD.
